summary: Daily Hacker News product scout; concrete, fresh items selected after full comment-tree coverage.
telegram_footer: 10 items surfaced | 25/25 stories scraped | history + run artifact updated

items.name: Bonsai 27B
items.source_story_title: Bonsai 27B: A 27B-Class Model that runs on a phone
items.summary: A heavily compressed 27B-class language model designed to run on phones.
items.why_included: A notable local-model release with unusually aggressive size claims and active hands-on discussion.
items.evidence: Commenters compared its ternary and 1-bit variants with other local models; some reported early compatibility and evaluation caveats.

items.name: Agnost AI
items.source_story_title: Launch HN: Agnost AI (YC S26) – Extract user feedback from agent conversations
items.summary: A system that extracts feedback themes and outliers from agent conversations.
items.why_included: It targets a concrete operational problem: identifying hidden requests and frustration across large conversation volumes.
items.evidence: The maker argued that keywords and SQL miss vague signals, while the product clusters themes and highlights outliers.

items.name: ai-trains-ai
items.source_story_title: Show HN: I RL-trained an agent that trains models with RL (for ~$1.3k)
items.summary: An open-source experiment where an RL agent writes RL training jobs.
items.why_included: A concrete, reproducible nested-RL prototype with a clear cost and limitation discussion.
items.evidence: Its author described 1,750 training jobs, hidden-eval feedback, and limits to small agentic tasks on the Prime-RL stack.

items.name: Juggler
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Juggler – an open-source GUI coding agent, by the creator of JUCE
items.summary: An open-source GUI coding agent organized around navigable conversation branches.
items.why_included: Its tree-based interaction model offers a concrete alternative to long, linear agent chat histories.
items.evidence: HN users praised the branching model and discussed worktrees, shared sessions, local models, and early setup issues.

items.name: Consent-O-Matic
items.source_story_title: European "age verification" "app" forcing everyone to use Android or iOS
items.summary: A browser extension that automatically applies your cookie-consent preferences.
items.why_included: It is a practical privacy utility surfaced as a direct response to consent-banner fatigue.
items.evidence: A commenter recommended it from personal use, saying it handles common dialogs and sharply reduces cookie popups.

items.name: TIC-80
items.source_story_title: European "age verification" "app" forcing everyone to use Android or iOS
items.summary: A free fantasy computer for making, sharing, and playing tiny retro games.
items.why_included: A concrete, approachable game-making alternative raised in a discussion about platform lock-in.
items.evidence: A parent described TIC-80 alongside Godot as a good, more forgiving option for kids making games.

items.name: Solar Sharer Calculator
items.source_story_title: Australian energy retailers must offer three hours of free daytime electricity
items.summary: A calculator for estimating savings from Australian free-daytime-power plans.
items.why_included: It turns a policy headline into a practical household decision tool.
items.evidence: Its creator said they built it to help someone assess whether switching to a three-hour free-power plan would save money.

items.name: Verba Prima
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Opening lines of famous literary works
items.summary: A browsable collection of opening lines from famous literary works.
items.why_included: A focused, lightweight cultural reference site that made the front-page cut.
items.evidence: The Show HN submission drew a substantial discussion thread around the presentation of literary openings.

items.name: 1 Million Minecraft World
items.source_story_title: The largest available Minecraft world, totalling 15 TB
items.summary: A 15 TB downloadable Minecraft world assembled from a million saved regions.
items.why_included: An unusually large preservation and exploration artifact for a long-running game world.
items.evidence: HN discussion engaged with the scale and availability of the 2b2t world archive.

items.name: Paxos Simplified
items.summary: A video-and-slides explanation of the Paxos distributed-consensus protocol.
items.why_included: It is a concrete learning aid for a famously difficult systems topic.
items.evidence: A commenter recommended it as a clearer companion to Lamport’s paper, alongside the MIT 6.824 labs.
